title: Remote monitors — MONITOR_P/DEMONITOR_P/MONITOR_P_EXIT
purpose: Cross-node monitors use MONITOR_P (establish), DEMONITOR_P (remove), and MONITOR_P_EXIT (DOWN notification) control messages. When a monitored process on node B exits, node B sends MONITOR_P_EXIT to node A, which delivers the DOWN message to the monitoring process.

requirements.title: Remote monitor establishment
requirements.spec: When monitor(process, RemotePid) is called, send MONITOR_P to the remote node.
acceptance: Outbound: monitor on remote PID sends MONITOR_P control with reference
Inbound: receiving MONITOR_P registers the monitor on the local process
If the target process is already dead, immediately send MONITOR_P_EXIT back
Test: monitor remote process, verify monitor established

requirements.title: Remote DOWN delivery
requirements.spec: When a monitored process exits, deliver MONITOR_P_EXIT to all remote monitors.
acceptance: Process exit triggers MONITOR_P_EXIT for each remote monitor
Remote node receives MONITOR_P_EXIT and delivers DOWN message to the monitoring process
DOWN message contains correct Ref, Pid, and Reason
Test: monitor remote process, it exits, verify DOWN received

requirements.title: Remote demonitor
acceptance: demonitor(Ref) for a remote monitor sends DEMONITOR_P to the remote node
Remote node removes the monitor registration
No DOWN sent after demonitor even if the process exits
Test: monitor, demonitor, process exits, no DOWN received

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement monitor by name across nodes — only by PID
SHALL handle node-down as a special DOWN reason (noconnection)
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
